div.head: Memorandum by the Assistant Chief of the Division of Eastern European Affairs (Durbrow)Addressed to Assistant Secretary of State Breckinridge Long and David M. Key, assistant liaison officer in the office of the Secretary of State.
div.p:
Pursuant to our conversation this morning I enclose herewith the original copy of a letterNot printed. received from Mr. KarpinskiZygmunt Karpinski, Director of the Bank of Poland. with which
he gives assurances that the Polish goldSee Foreign Relations, 1943, vol. iii, p. 443, and footnote 4, p. 444. to be transported to the United States is ready for shipment from Dakar and that arrangements have been made with the Federal Reserve Bank to insure each shipment for the amount of $3,125,000.The total amount of gold to be shipped was held in 445 cases containing gold bars and coin valued at approximately $27,200,000.

I have been assured by Mr. Kwapiszewski,Michal Kwapiszewski, Counselor of the Polish Embassy in Washington, who held the rank of Minister Plenipotentiary. the Chargé d’Affaires a.i., that he will execute the waiver of responsibility certificate and send it to me within the next two days.
You may care to pass this information on to the Navy Department.
